title: Select Columns
description: Use dplyr's select() to pick specific columns from a data frame.
problemStatement:
Tidy selection! Load `library(dplyr)`. Given a data frame `df <- data.frame(ID=1, Name="R", Score=100)`, use `select()` to keep only `Name` and `Score`.

**Requirement:** Use the `%>%` pipe if possible, but at least use `select()`.
hints: `select()` works with column names without quotes.
solutionCode:
library(dplyr)
df <- data.frame(ID=1, Name="R", Score=100)
result <- df %>% select(Name, Score)
print(result)
